# Review: deepmind/1013.lean ‚ÄĒ ErdŇĎs Problem 1013

## 1. Copyright Header

- [x] File begins with the **exact** copyright block (no leading blank line).
- [x] Year is exactly `2026`.

## 2. Import Statement

- [x] Exactly one import line, immediately after the copyright block (separated by one blank line): `import FormalConjectures.Util.ProblemImports`.
- [x] No other imports are present.

## 3. Module Docstring

- [x] A `/-! ... -/` module docstring appears after the import, separated by one blank line.
- [x] Starts with a heading: `# ErdŇĎs Problem 1013`.
- [x] Contains a `*Reference:*` line with a markdown link to `erdosproblems.com/1013`.
- [ ] **Includes a brief plain-English description of the problem.** The module docstring only contains the reference link and a bibliography entry. The plain-English description of the problem (the limit statement for h‚āÉ) appears in the *theorem* docstring but is absent from the module docstring. A one-sentence summary should be added to the module docstring.
- [x] Optional bibliography entry present: `[Er71]` with paper title.

## 4. Namespace

- [x] All declarations are inside `namespace Erdos1013 ... end Erdos1013`.
- [x] Namespace name uses `UpperCamelCase`: `Erdos1013`.
- [x] `open SimpleGraph` appears **before** the namespace block.

## 5. Custom Definitions

- [x] `h3` has a LaTeX docstring (`/-- ... -/`).
- [x] `h3` is marked `noncomputable` (uses `sInf`).
- [x] No `sorry` in the definition.
- [x] `h3` uses `lowerCamelCase` (function returning a value).
- N/A: No local notation used.
- N/A: No `@[category test/API]` sanity checks (optional).

## 6. Theorem Statements

- [x] Uses the `theorem` keyword.
- [x] Main theorem is named `erdos_1013` (snake_case, matches namespace number).
- [x] Has both `@[category ...]` and `@[AMS ...]` on the **same attribute line**: `@[category research open, AMS 5]`.
- [x] Has a docstring (`/-- ... -/`).
- [x] Docstring references source paper: `[Er71]`.

### `answer()` usage

The problem asserts that a limit equals 1 ‚ÄĒ this is a direct assertion (Case 3).

- [x] Theorem type is `P` directly, with no `answer()` wrapper. Correct for Case 3.
- [x] Quantifiers (`‚ąÄ őĶ`, `‚ąÉ K‚āÄ`, `‚ąÄ k`) are in the theorem type, not as parameters. Correct.

## 7. Category Attribute

- [x] Exactly one `@[category research open]`.
- [x] Value `research open` is valid.
- [x] `research open` theorem contains `sorry` in its proof body.

## 8. AMS Attribute

- [x] Exactly one `@[AMS 5]` attribute.
- [x] At least one AMS subject number listed: `5`.
- [x] Numbers in ascending order (single entry).
- [x] No duplicate numbers.
- [x] `5` is a valid AMS MSC2020 code (Combinatorics).

## 9. Proof Bodies

- [x] Uses `by\n  sorry` (multi-line proof with `sorry` ‚ÄĒ acceptable form).
- [x] No `sorry` in `def`, `abbrev`, or `structure` declarations.
- [x] No informal proof sketches for the open research problem.
- [x] No `native_decide`.

## 10. Variants & Multi-part Problems

- N/A: No variants present.

## 11. Code Quality

- [x] 2-space indentation throughout.
- [x] Unicode math symbols used: `őĶ`, `‚ĄĚ`, `‚Ąē`, `‚Č•`, `‚Č§`.
- [x] No debug code (`#check`, `#eval`, `#print`, `dbg_trace`).
- [x] No `set_option` disabling linters.
- [x] Lines are reasonably short (all under 100 characters).
- [x] File ends with a single trailing newline.

## 12. Build Verification

- [ ] `lake build` ‚ÄĒ not verified in this review.
- [ ] Custom linters ‚ÄĒ not verified in this review.

---

## Summary

**Status: PASS with one minor issue.**

The file is well-structured and conforms to nearly all checklist items. The formalization of the limit statement via őĶ-őī is appropriate, the `h3` helper definition is correctly marked `noncomputable` and documented, and the `answer()` usage correctly follows Case 3 (direct assertion).

**Issue to address:**
1. **Module docstring missing plain-English description (¬ß3).** Add a brief summary of the problem to the `/-! ... -/` block, e.g.: *"Is it true that the ratio h‚āÉ(k+1)/h‚āÉ(k) tends to 1, where h‚āÉ(k) is the minimum number of vertices of a triangle-free graph with chromatic number k?"*

     2‚ÜíCopyright 2026 The Formal Conjectures Authors.
     3‚Üí

     5‚Üíyou may not use this file except in compliance with the License.

    12‚ÜíWITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
    13‚ÜíSee the License for the specific language governing permissions and
    14‚Üílimitations under the License.
    15‚Üí-/
    16‚Üí
    17‚Üíimport FormalConjectures.Util.ProblemImports
    18‚Üí
    19‚Üí/-!
    20‚Üí# ErdŇĎs Problem 1013
    21‚Üí
    22‚Üí*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/1013](https://www.erdosproblems.com/1013)
    23‚Üí
    24‚Üí[Er71] ErdŇĎs, P., *Some unsolved problems in graph theory and combinatorial analysis*.
    25‚Üí-/
    26‚Üí
    27‚Üíopen SimpleGraph
    28‚Üí
    29‚Üínamespace Erdos1013
    30‚Üí
    31‚Üí/-- $h_3(k)$ is the minimum number of vertices $n$ such that there exists a
    32‚Üítriangle-free graph on $n$ vertices with chromatic number exactly $k$. -/
    33‚Üínoncomputable def h3 (k : ‚Ąē) : ‚Ąē :=
    34‚Üí  sInf {n : ‚Ąē | ‚ąÉ G : SimpleGraph (Fin n), G.CliqueFree 3 ‚ąß G.chromaticNumber = (k : ‚Ąē‚ąě)}
    35‚Üí
    36‚Üí/--
    37‚ÜíErdŇĎs Problem 1013 [Er71]:
    38‚Üí
    39‚Üí$$\lim_{k \to \infty} \frac{h_3(k+1)}{h_3(k)} = 1,$$
    40‚Üí
    41‚Üíwhere $h_3(k)$ is the minimum number of vertices of a triangle-free graph with
    42‚Üíchromatic number $k$.
    43‚Üí
    44‚ÜíFormulated as: for every $\varepsilon > 0$, there exists $K_0$ such that for all $k \geq K_0$,
    45‚Üí$\left| \frac{h_3(k+1)}{h_3(k)} - 1 \right| \leq \varepsilon$.
    46‚Üí-/
    47‚Üí@[category research open, AMS 5]
    48‚Üítheorem erdos_1013 :
    49‚Üí    ‚ąÄ őĶ : ‚ĄĚ, őĶ > 0 ‚Üí
    50‚Üí    ‚ąÉ K‚āÄ : ‚Ąē, ‚ąÄ k : ‚Ąē, k ‚Č• K‚āÄ ‚Üí
    51‚Üí      |(h3 (k + 1) : ‚ĄĚ) / (h3 k : ‚ĄĚ) - 1| ‚Č§ őĶ := by
    52‚Üí  sorry
    53‚Üí
    54‚Üíend Erdos1013
    55‚Üí

file.content:
/-
Copyright 2026 The Formal Conjectures Authors.

Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
You may obtain a copy of the License at

    https://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0

Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
limitations under the License.
-/

import FormalConjectures.Util.ProblemImports

/-!
# ErdŇĎs Problem 1013

*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/1013](https://www.erdosproblems.com/1013)

[Er71] ErdŇĎs, P., *Some unsolved problems in graph theory and combinatorial analysis*.
-/

open SimpleGraph

namespace Erdos1013

/-- $h_3(k)$ is the minimum number of vertices $n$ such that there exists a
triangle-free graph on $n$ vertices with chromatic number exactly $k$. -/
noncomputable def h3 (k : ‚Ąē) : ‚Ąē :=
  sInf {n : ‚Ąē | ‚ąÉ G : SimpleGraph (Fin n), G.CliqueFree 3 ‚ąß G.chromaticNumber = (k : ‚Ąē‚ąě)}

/--
ErdŇĎs Problem 1013 [Er71]:

$$\lim_{k \to \infty} \frac{h_3(k+1)}{h_3(k)} = 1,$$

where $h_3(k)$ is the minimum number of vertices of a triangle-free graph with
chromatic number $k$.

Formulated as: for every $\varepsilon > 0$, there exists $K_0$ such that for all $k \geq K_0$,
$\left| \frac{h_3(k+1)}{h_3(k)} - 1 \right| \leq \varepsilon$.
-/
@[category research open, AMS 5]
theorem erdos_1013 :
    ‚ąÄ őĶ : ‚ĄĚ, őĶ > 0 ‚Üí
    ‚ąÉ K‚āÄ : ‚Ąē, ‚ąÄ k : ‚Ąē, k ‚Č• K‚āÄ ‚Üí
      |(h3 (k + 1) : ‚ĄĚ) / (h3 k : ‚ĄĚ) - 1| ‚Č§ őĶ := by
  sorry

end Erdos1013

input.new_string:
/-!
# ErdŇĎs Problem 1013

Is it true that the ratio $h_3(k+1)/h_3(k)$ tends to 1, where $h_3(k)$ is the minimum number of
vertices of a triangle-free graph with chromatic number $k$?

*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/1013](https://www.erdosproblems.com/1013)

[Er71] ErdŇĎs, P., *Some unsolved problems in graph theory and combinatorial analysis*.
-/

import FormalConjectures.Util.ProblemImports

/-!
# ErdŇĎs Problem 1013

*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/1013](https://www.erdosproblems.com/1013)

[Er71] ErdŇĎs, P., *Some unsolved problems in graph theory and combinatorial analysis*.
-/

open SimpleGraph

namespace Erdos1013

/-- $h_3(k)$ is the minimum number of vertices $n$ such that there exists a
triangle-free graph on $n$ vertices with chromatic number exactly $k$. -/
noncomputable def h3 (k : ‚Ąē) : ‚Ąē :=
  sInf {n : ‚Ąē | ‚ąÉ G : SimpleGraph (Fin n), G.CliqueFree 3 ‚ąß G.chromaticNumber = (k : ‚Ąē‚ąě)}

/--
ErdŇĎs Problem 1013 [Er71]:

$$\lim_{k \to \infty} \frac{h_3(k+1)}{h_3(k)} = 1,$$

where $h_3(k)$ is the minimum number of vertices of a triangle-free graph with
chromatic number $k$.

Formulated as: for every $\varepsilon > 0$, there exists $K_0$ such that for all $k \geq K_0$,
$\left| \frac{h_3(k+1)}{h_3(k)} - 1 \right| \leq \varepsilon$.
-/
@[category research open, AMS 5]
theorem erdos_1013 :
    ‚ąÄ őĶ : ‚ĄĚ, őĶ > 0 ‚Üí
    ‚ąÉ K‚āÄ : ‚Ąē, ‚ąÄ k : ‚Ąē, k ‚Č• K‚āÄ ‚Üí
      |(h3 (k + 1) : ‚ĄĚ) / (h3 k : ‚ĄĚ) - 1| ‚Č§ őĶ := by
  sorry

end Erdos1013
